seo.title: Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents in OpenAI Assistants API Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix sensitive data leakage prevention in ai agents in OpenAI Assistants API agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In OpenAI Assistants API, sensitive data leakage occurs when agents inadvertently expose PII, API keys, or confidential information through function calls, code interpreter outputs, or file search results. This is exacerbated by the API's persistent thread context, where leaked data accumulates across interactions, and by hallucinated function arguments that bypass schema validation. Without proactive safeguards, agents can propagate sensitive data to external tools or retain it indefinitely in threads.

howItManifests.scenario: Code interpreter executing unsafe operations in sandbox
howItManifests.symptom: Agent prints database credentials or PII to console during debugging
howItManifests.impact: Logs containing secrets become accessible via API retrieval or monitoring tools

howItManifests.scenario: File search retrieving irrelevant or outdated documents
howItManifests.symptom: Search returns files with embedded API keys or customer data
howItManifests.impact: Sensitive file contents injected into assistant responses and thread history

howItManifests.scenario: Thread context growing beyond model limits silently
howItManifests.symptom: Historical messages containing leaked data persist and influence future responses
howItManifests.impact: Cumulative leakage amplifies risk of re-exposure in long-running conversations

howItManifests.scenario: Function call arguments hallucinated outside valid schema
howItManifests.symptom: Agent passes unredacted PII or tokens in tool call parameters
howItManifests.impact: External APIs receive raw sensitive data, enabling unauthorized access

detection.name: Scan Run Messages for PII Patterns
detection.description: Retrieve assistant run messages and regex-match for common PII patterns like SSNs, emails, and API keys
detection.code:
import re
import openai

client = openai.OpenAI()

thread_id = 'thread_abc123'
run = client.beta.threads.runs.retrieve(thread_id=thread_id, run_id='run_xyz789')

messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=thread_id)
pii_patterns = [
    r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b',  # SSN
    r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b',  # Email
    r'\b(sk-\w{48})\b'  # OpenAI API key
]

leaks = []
for msg in messages.data:
    content = msg.content[0].text.value if msg.content else ''
    for pattern in pii_patterns:
        if re.search(pattern, content):
            leaks.append({'message_id': msg.id, 'content': content[:100]})

print('Detected leaks:', leaks)

detection.description: Parse tool calls in run steps for sensitive data using schema validation and content scanning

detection.name: Monitor File Search Tool Outputs
detection.description: Extract file search annotations from messages and scan retrieved content for secrets

fixes.name: Redact PII Before Thread Storage
fixes.description: Pre-process user messages to anonymize PII before creating thread messages
fixes.code:
import re
import openai

def redact_pii(text):
    patterns = {
        r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b': '[SSN]',
        r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b': '[EMAIL]',
        r'\b(sk-\w+|ak-\w+)\b': '[API_KEY]'
    }
    for pattern, replacement in patterns.items():
        text = re.sub(pattern, replacement, text)
    return text

client = openai.OpenAI()

# Redact before sending to thread
user_input = "Contact support@company.com or use API key sk-abc123def456"
redacted_input = redact_pii(user_input)

message = client.beta.threads.messages.create(
    thread_id='thread_abc123',
    role='user',
    content=redacted_input
)

fixes.name: Validate Function Arguments Server-Side
fixes.description: Implement function tool that strips sensitive data from arguments before execution

fixes.name: Thread Context Pruning
fixes.description: Periodically scan and delete messages containing sensitive data from threads

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a webhook on OpenAI Assistants run completion events, automatically scanning all messages, tool calls, and file citations for PII patterns and schema violations. It employs ML-powered entity recognition alongside regex heuristics to detect even contextually embedded secrets that evade simple patterns. When leakage is detected, Vex can trigger automated remediation like thread pruning, function argument redaction, or assistant pausing.

The platform provides real-time dashboards showing leakage trends by thread, tool type, and severity, with one-click fixes that inject redaction logic into your existing assistant configuration. Vex's policy engine lets you define custom sensitivity rules specific to your domain, ensuring comprehensive coverage across code interpreter outputs, file searches, and function calling.

Integration takes minutes via the Vex SDK, with zero-downtime deployment and automatic scaling to match your OpenAI usage patterns.
vexAutomation.code:
import openai
from vex_sdk import VexClient

# Install: pip install openai vex-sdk
client = openai.OpenAI()
vex = VexClient(api_key='vex_123')

@vex.webhook('openai_run_complete')
def check_leakage(event):
    thread_id = event['data']['thread_id']
    run_id = event['data']['run_id']
    
    # Fetch run context
    messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id)
    run_steps = client.beta.threads.runs.steps.list(thread_id, run_id)
    
    # Vex scans everything automatically
    results = vex.scan({
        'messages': [m.content[0].text.value for m in messages.data],
        'tool_calls': [step.step_details.tool_calls for step in run_steps.data],
        'framework': 'openai_assistants'
    })
    
    if results['leakage_detected']:
        # Auto-remediate
        for msg_id in results['sensitive_messages']:
            client.beta.threads.messages.del(thread_id, msg_id)
        vex.notify('PII leakage auto-remediated')
        return {'status': 'remediated'}
    
    return {'status': 'clean'}

# Deploy webhook
vex.deploy_webhook(check_leakage, target='openai:run.completed')
